FLOW.COMMENT: vfSessionLastError - this is a framework only service to perform lastError processing - call from the catch block immediately after getLastError

SEQUENCE.COMMENT: processLastError - perform lastError processing - call from the catch block immediately after getLastError

INVOKE.COMMENT: sDatetime = yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.SSSZ in UTC ie 2018-08-01 17:57:23.287+0000
look for FWTRANSEND which is a special 'error' case where the caller just wants to end the transaction before all steps are completed

BRANCH.COMMENT: if we find FWTRANSEND in the error message, exit quietly

INVOKE.COMMENT: Exiting from processLastError after finding FWTRANSEND at position %value%

Values.value: Exiting from processLastError after finding FWTRANSEND at position %value%

SEQUENCE.COMMENT: add a call stack to the body of the message

INVOKE.COMMENT: stack - this generates an abbreviated, single string stack

MAP.COMMENT: get sSize as the number of items in the call stack
extract the system-generated callStack separately from lastError

LOOP.COMMENT: this loop generates an easier-to-view, nested stack trace in a human-readable format

INVOKE.COMMENT: lastError pipeline has been saved - use restorePipeline ( %sFwTransactionid%-lastError ) to view

Values.value: lastError pipeline has been saved - use restorePipeline ( %sFwTransactionid%-lastError ) to view

MAP.COMMENT: note that the following map doesnt use global variable substitution, since it apparently interferes with the subsequent smtp call
sBody = %sBody%%sMessage% Full log can be found in the /csutil/framework/transaction/logs/... folder

Values.value:
%sBody% %sMessage%
Full log can be found in the /csutil/framework/transaction/logs/... folder

SEQUENCE.COMMENT: update the TFW_TRANS_ACTIVE table with a FAILED status - note that this is an EXIT ON DONE sequence

MAP.COMMENT: extract sErrnum and sErrtext from sErrmsg using regex based on the first space

SEQUENCE.COMMENT: create a USD ticket - note that this is also an EXIT ON DONE sequence

MAP.COMMENT: extract sErrnum and sErrtext from sErrmsgSub using regex based on the first space

INVOKE.COMMENT: FRAMEWORK, sErrnum, sBody, Transaction failure on %sFwTransactionid% %sErrmsg%
